title: The Ground Where Shadow Keeps Its Shape

features.name: the cast mark
features.desc-on-touch: The shadow of a fence post burned into the earth decades ago. When you touch it, the soil is still warm with the shape of light that fell so long it learned to stay.

features.name: the shadow-keeper
features.desc-on-touch: A figure that casts no light and holds three shadows—not its own. The archive noticed it first by the absence of confusion.

features.name: the darkening contour
features.desc-on-touch: The ground rises and falls in the exact shape of the largest shadow this place has ever held. The grass remembers; the earth knows.

features.name: the margin-place
features.desc-on-touch: Where the shadow ends, the ground begins. You stand at the line and find you cast two shadows now—one that moves and one that doesn't.

inscription: the shadow has learned its weight; the ground has learned to hold it

file.name: Fence_Post_Burn_Pattern_Narrabri_1962.tif
file.note: A shadow-mark on earth that persists though the fence has long been removed. The soil analysis dates it three years older than the photograph.

exits.label: back through the sharpening edge where light begins again
further into the lengthening dusk where shadows merge with the ground they rest on
up the contour toward the place where the shadow first fell
